purpose: Validate whether San Jacinto road geometry is available to consumer-language surfaces or whether community-level location still wins over roadway context.

validatedIncidentModel.source: V650R.11 browser-observed San Jacinto road-hazard presentation
validatedIncidentModel.ownership: san-jacinto-tx via validation-only awareness area and county runtime registration

incidentLocationTrace.finding: The browser-visible model is a San Jacinto crash/wreck with Coldspring community text and validation coordinates at the Coldspring awareness anchor.
San Jacinto remains validation-only and blocked from production activation; the Coldspring awareness area is scoped to san-jacinto-tx.
collectNearbyRoadCandidates uses the active county roadwaySegmentFeatures dataset after San Jacinto runtime wiring; static candidate trace found roadway geometry within 0.45 miles.
resolveNearestRoadName can select the closest valid roadway segment and can append a meaningful secondary road through resolveNearbyRoadPair.

resolveIncidentRoadLookupPayload appends resolved road geometry after explicit incident location fields; explicit community fields can therefore win before the resolver output when they are treated as resolvable location candidates.

buildGridlyAlertCardConsumerModel normalizes the event title to Crash / Wreck but does not remove a pre-rendered community location from fallback title unless a stronger authoritative road label is present in the alert record.

normalizeGridlyAlertCardLocationLabel checks authoritative road-hazard labels first, then popup/community fields; if the record has only locationName/knownLocation=Coldspring and no road fields, detail language can remain community-oriented.

Lightweight awareness resolves active item category and location from supplied active hazard/report fields; it does not independently force the nearest roadway resolver over explicit community labels.

roadCandidateFindings.method: Static local GeoJSON distance trace against assets/county-implementation/san-jacinto/runtime-assets/source/san-jacinto-county-road-segments.geojson at 30.5924,-95.1294 with 0.45 mile radius.

roadResolutionFindings.secondaryRoadAvailable: State Hwy 156 or State Hwy 150, depending source feature order after same-distance sorting
roadResolutionFindings.selectionScore: distance-based nearest valid candidate; closest distance approximately 0.0081 miles (about 13 meters)
roadResolutionFindings.acceptedOrRejected: Accepted by resolver when coordinates and San Jacinto roadwaySegmentFeatures are loaded; not guaranteed to win consumer text if explicit community fields enter the location-candidate list earlier.

communityPreferenceFindings.intentionalPolicy: Partly intentional as a fallback/explicit-field preference, not as a San Jacinto-specific community-first rule.
communityPreferenceFindings.expectedBehavior: Crash / Wreck on Coldspring is consumer-friendly but not the most specific behavior when nearby road geometry and a valid roadway name are available.
communityPreferenceFindings.recommendationClass: B. Road resolution available but location-selection logic needs adjustment

liberty.behavior: Liberty has roadSegmentsPath available and the same resolver can preserve resolved road names; community fallback is used when no useful road label is present.
liberty.parityExpectation: San Jacinto should match Liberty: road name first when a valid resolved road label is available, community fallback only when road resolution is unavailable or invalid.

montgomery.behavior: Montgomery roadSegmentsPath remains null, so safe selected-awareness-area/county wording is expected when no road resolves; valid explicit Montgomery road names are preserved.
montgomery.parityExpectation: San Jacinto should not mirror Montgomery fallback behavior now that San Jacinto road geometry is available.

consumerLanguageReview.standard: Accepted language requires clear consumer wording without technical metadata and with an understandable location phrase.
consumerLanguageReview.notes: The phrase is understandable and consumer-safe, but it is less specific than available road context for the validated coordinate. Do not redesign wording in this milestone.

roadCandidateLookup.notes: Nearby roadway candidates are found from San Jacinto geometry.

roadResolution.notes: A valid roadway name is available and distance-selectable.

locationPhraseGeneration.notes: Explicit community fields can precede resolver-derived roadway labels.

alertTitle.notes: Community-oriented fallback title can remain visible.

alertDetail.notes: Detail line can use community label when road fields are absent from alert record.

awarenessHeadline.notes: Awareness uses reusable active item location fields and does not force road geometry over community fields.

libertyComparison.notes: Liberty road-first parity indicates San Jacinto should use roads when available.

montgomeryComparison.notes: Montgomery fallback is expected because road geometry remains missing.

consumerLanguage.notes: Consumer-safe but less specific than available road evidence.

remainingBlockers.item: Location-selection precedence can allow Coldspring to win over available roadway context.
Boundary credibility remains tracked separately and is not resolved by this milestone.

mergeRecommendation: Merge as documentation-only validation. No activation, production behavior change, San Jacinto-specific wording rule, Liberty behavior change, Montgomery behavior change, or boundary asset change is included.
